question.question_text: A car is traveling along a straight rode at a constant speed of 100mph for 5 hours calculate the total distance traveled
question.question_html: A car is traveling along a straight road at a constant speed of {{params.v}} {{params.unitsSpeed}} for {{params.t}} {{params.unitsTime}}. Calculate the total distance traveled.

files.question.html: A car is traveling along a straight road at a constant speed of {{params.v}} {{params.unitsSpeed}} for {{params.t}} {{params.unitsTime}}. Calculate the total distance traveled.
files.solution.html:
<pl-solution-panel>
    <pl-hint level="1" data-type="text">
        The car is traveling at a constant speed of {{params.v}} {{params.unitsSpeed}} for a duration of {{params.t}} {{params.unitsTime}}. To find the total distance traveled, we can use the basic formula for distance in uniform motion.
    </pl-hint>
    <pl-hint level="2" data-type="text">
        The formula for distance (
        $d$) when speed is constant is represented as:
        $$ d = vt $$
    </pl-hint>
    <pl-hint level="3" data-type="text">
        Substituting the known variables into our equation, we have:
        $$ d = {{params.v}} \times {{params.t}} $$
    </pl-hint>
    <pl-hint level="4" data-type="text">
        Therefore, the total distance traveled by the car is given by:
        $$ d = {{params.v}} \times {{params.t}} = {{correct_answers.d}} {{params.unitsDistance}} $$
    </pl-hint>
</pl-solution-panel>
